page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by Intel Xeon processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 512 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The instance achieves a strong multi-core score of 120,717.34 in stress-ng and 16,975.0 in Geekbench. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test reaching a top-tier score of 104,777.43, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Memory bandwidth is also robust, measuring 309.76 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 135.09 GB/sec for large block write operations. Ray tracing performance is top-tier, scoring 106,453.0 on Geekbench. However, single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,541.0.

The n2-standard-128 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise-scale workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of local NVMe storage means all disk I/O relies on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff for disk-heavy applications. LLM inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium models, but text generation for large 70B models is weak at 2.7 tokens/second, highlighting the limitations of CPU-only inference for large-scale AI. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale parallel computing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance configured with 128 vCPUs of Intel Xeon processors running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 512 GB of RAM. It features a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 120,717.34 and top-tier cryptographic performance. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 309.76 GB/sec for large block reads. Single-core performance and large-model LLM text generation are moderate to weak, representing a clear tradeoff. This instance is highly efficient for parallelized workloads, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is a general-purpose server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance offering balanced memory allocation, strong multi-threaded performance, and advanced cryptographic acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM powered by Intel Xeon processors
Strong multi-core performance with top-tier cryptographic acceleration
High memory bandwidth of up to 309.76 GB/sec for large block reads
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Well-suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
